pay_metric: NIRF grad-weighted median salary; progression via PLFS Engineering x1.29 / x1.57
pay_source: NIRF 2025 (nirf_fact_aggregate, BQ) + PLFS CY2025 age-band growth
notes: All IITs reporting a median + grad count to NIRF 2025. Progression assumes IIT grads track the PLFS Engineering growth curve; the true top-quartile (FAANG/MBA) is steeper. Non-placed are largely higher-studies, not unemployed.

pay_metric: NIRF grad-weighted median salary; progression via PLFS Engineering x1.29 / x1.57
pay_source: NIRF 2025 (nirf_fact_aggregate, BQ) + PLFS CY2025 age-band growth
notes: NITs + IIITs reporting to NIRF 2025. Non-placed are largely higher-studies, not unemployed.

pay_metric: NIRF grad-weighted median salary; progression via PLFS Engineering x1.29 / x1.57
pay_source: NIRF 2025 (nirf_fact_aggregate, BQ) + PLFS CY2025 age-band growth
notes: Engineering institutes ranked <=200 in NIRF 2025, excluding IIT/NIT/IIIT (BITS, VIT, COEP, Anna Univ campuses, etc.). Non-placed are largely higher-studies, not unemployed.

rate_source: Compulsory internship + govt service — ~all govt-seat MBBS/BDS practise
pay_metric: Govt junior-resident scale at start; progression via PLFS Medical x1.30 / x1.33
pay_source: 7th CPC junior-resident scale (~₹56k/mo) + PLFS CY2025 Medical growth
notes: ~60,000 govt MBBS+BDS seats (NMC/DCI, approx). PLFS Medical pools MBBS with nurses/AYUSH/pharma, so age progression UNDER-counts true specialist trajectory (post-PG ₹15-25 L).

bucket: 5. All other STEM grads (non-elite engineering + IT/Computer + non-MBBS medical)
rate_source: PLFS CY2025 % in regular work @25-29, non-technical graduate+PG
pay_metric: PLFS non-technical graduate+PG regular-salaried median (incl. B.Sc science)
pay_source: PLFS CY2025 directly (this bucket IS the PLFS cohort — no NIRF anchor)
notes: The modal 'ordinary graduate' counterfactual: a non-elite student becomes a general/science graduate. Pay & % in regular work from the PLFS non-technical graduate+PG group (the science/general-degree cohort). grads = all STEM UG minus the four ranked/govt tiers. NOT blended with engineering (that overstates the rest: STEM-weighted blend ≈ ₹3.1 L).
